inline_comments.message:
@Toine, What should be the attribute name here as it supposed to be data as per proposed document?

I used here as "responsedata" to avoid any confusion as "data" is already used before at line no. 2
i have not tested it but in my opinion the fields inside the data ( payload ) can still follow the normal camel casing convention as it was before.
I think we agreed to remove response isn't it? (if you included the batch string removal in this commit)
In cloud Events approach, I think we need to keep this '.withExtension'. So, it should be in lower case and appended with 'ce_'

Its patch #1 that contains only -> Removal of existing header schema and renaming of events.
Pathc #2 will contain these changes.
I don't mind so much I think e did agree to remove it...
in any case we want consistency; either remove the word 'Response' everywhere (like here) or add it again..
yes we discussed this, see #17 here: https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS+Events+Structure#CPSEventsStructure-Issues&Decisions
NB: I dont thin the word 'async' is needed
as dciussed last week with Ravi, just either 'include' or 'exclude' to be clear
but if the header schema is removed we dont have the header you are checking here?!
question: does this test show the working of the filter or not ie. could we write a simple test to see an event is included or not depending on the properties used to filter?!
good question. maybe 'responseDetails' or 'responsContent' > Fire a quick mail to Kieran/Tony for advise... (but first add a commetn on teh relevant wiki and point to the wiki-section in teh email)
We followed this pattern for all other classes as well. For consistency I did not renamed it.
def 'Filter an event with type #eventType'() test does this.

please express as Include....  
So I assume this one would be IncludeDataOperationEventsOnly

I was thinking about this (in any case it shoudl be 1_0_0)
We can decide when we support multiple version whether we handle it in the same consumer or not...
as discussed yesreday you test teh filter logic but not teh annoaation. Cna you add a test using kafak(Container?) taht wil verify teh (filter) annoatation work as expected
Please add versioning to the package as we discussed
To support this filter for all versions just remove this versioning from here.

If we use this here this filter only works for the version 1_0_0. May be we need to think to update the eventType header value checking. For example for the version 2_0_0 the evenType header value will be 'org.onap.cps.ncmp.events.async2_0_0.DataOperationEvent2_0_0, then in this case filter excludes the new version event.
To support this filter for all versions of type 'org.onap.cps.ncmp.events.async1_0_0.DataOperationEvent' just remove this versioning from here.

NB: May be we can add one more test case after line 88 with description= 'not filtered(the consumer will see the event)', eventType= 'org.onap.cps.ncmp.events.async2_0_0.DataOperationEvent2_0_0' and expectedResult= 'false' Once we update the filterstrategy eventTypeHeader value checking and moved to cloud event.
how come the package now also has a version?!
'starstWith' this wont work anymore as intended if the (same ?!) version is in the package name too ?!
use Jacoco report to ensure all paths are covered....
we agreed NOT to add package version to package names ?!
we agreed to use version with pkg name only.
Removed : javaType is not required here.
U will be moving to CloudEvents in the next iteration ??
I think, there is an other commit which does the same, at the moment it's in WIP with sourabh.
Yes, Patch# 3 will replace it with CloudEvents.
